title: Imagist haiku — 'White feather, iron rails'
prompt: Three-line English haiku, imagist constraint (concrete sensory specificity).
constraint: imagism: at least one concrete sensory noun per line; no abstractions in line 1.
notes: Highest-composite Phase 7 cascade output for poetry_gen (composite ≈ 0.635). Honest reading: the vimarśa event did NOT fire on this item, so the cascade committed the draft. The shadow-revision pass produced 'Sings when trains pass through' — slightly different image but the proxy scorer rates it 0.59 vs the draft's 0.64, so the event-gate's decision to keep the draft was *correct* by the scorer. This is an example of the gate working as designed.
